questions.question: How do you remove default list bullets?
questions.explanation: list-style: none removes all list markers (bullets/numbers).

questions.question: Which property makes list items display horizontally?
options: display: inline on li elements or display: flex on ul/ol
questions.explanation: Set display: inline or use flexbox on the parent ul/ol to create horizontal lists.

questions.question: What does list-style-position: inside do?
options: Places the marker inside the content box, so text wraps under the marker
Moves the list inside the parent
questions.explanation: list-style-position: inside places the bullet/number inside the content area, so text wraps under the marker.

questions.question: Which list-style-type value creates uppercase Roman numerals?
questions.explanation: list-style-type: upper-roman displays I, II, III, IV for ordered lists.

questions.question: What is the ::marker pseudo-element used for?
questions.explanation: ::marker allows styling the list item marker (bullet or number) with color, font-size, and other properties.

questions.question: How do you create a custom counter in CSS?
questions.explanation: CSS counters use counter-reset (initialize), counter-increment (increase), and content with counter() to display custom numbering.

questions.question: What is the default list-style-type for unordered lists?
questions.explanation: The default marker for unordered lists is 'disc' (filled circle).

questions.question: What is the shorthand order for list-style?
questions.explanation: The list-style shorthand order is: list-style: type position image. Any values can be omitted.

questions.question: How do you use an image as a list marker?
questions.explanation: list-style-image replaces the default marker with a custom image.

questions.question: How do you create a description list in HTML?
questions.explanation: Description lists use <dl> (list), <dt> (term), and <dd> (description) elements, styled separately.

questions.question: What does list-style-type: decimal-leading-zero do?
options: Adds leading zeros to all numbers
Creates a numbered list without leading zeros
questions.explanation: decimal-leading-zero numbers ordered list items as 01, 02, 03, etc., padding single digits with a leading zero.

questions.question: What is list-style-position: outside (default)?
options: Marker is inside the content box
Marker is outside the content box, hanging to the left
questions.explanation: list-style-position: outside places the marker to the left of the content, hanging outside the element.

questions.question: How do you create a nested list with different bullet styles per level?
options: Use different list-style-type on ul, ul ul, ul ul ul
Use a single list-style-type on the parent
Nested lists can't have different styles
questions.explanation: Apply list-style-type to nested selectors (ul { disc }, ul ul { circle }, ul ul ul { square }) for hierarchical bullets.

questions.question: What does counter-increment do?
options: Increases a counter's value by a specified amount
questions.explanation: counter-increment increases the value of a named counter by 1 (or a specified amount).

questions.question: Which display value is commonly used for creating horizontal navigation from lists?
questions.explanation: Either display: inline/inline-block on list items or display: flex on the parent ul creates horizontal navigation lists.

questions.question: How can you change the marker for a single list item?
options: Use list-style on the li element
questions.explanation: The value attribute on an <li> changes its number in an ordered list, e.g., <li value="10">.

questions.question: What is the best way to create a custom numbered list with prefixes like Step 1, Step 2?
options: Use CSS counters with the counter() function and a string prefix
Write each number manually in HTML
questions.explanation: CSS counters allow custom formatting: content: 'Step ' counter(my-counter) to display 'Step 1', 'Step 2', etc.

questions.question: What does list-style inherit from parent elements?
options: list-style-type, list-style-position, and list-style-image are all inherited
questions.explanation: All list-style sub-properties are inherited. Nested lists inherit settings from their parent unless overridden.

questions.question: How do you add custom content before each list item?
questions.explanation: The ::before pseudo-element on li elements allows adding custom content before each item.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the start attribute on <ol>?
options: Sets the starting number for an ordered list
Restarts numbering from a specific value
questions.explanation: The start attribute on <ol> sets the starting number, e.g., <ol start="5"> starts numbering from 5.
